method_atom: An iterative message-passing inference scheme on a densely connected network of coupled variables, in which the many microscopic pairwise messages are replaced by condensed messages representing their macroscopic averages, making marginal estimation tractable. The method is extended from regimes where the solution space is a single contiguous basin to regimes where it fragments into multiple disconnected components, and it yields a highly efficient practical algorithm.
object_atom: The multiuser signal-detection problem in Code Division Multiple Access (CDMA) communication systems, where transmitted symbols must be inferred from densely overlapping received signals.
brief_reason: The condensed-message / macroscopic-average inference machinery for densely coupled variables (including the fragmented-solution-space extension) is the transferable method, while CDMA signal detection is the communications-specific object it is demonstrated on.
